notes: Bronze DQ rules for raw.onet_experience (O*NET Education, Training, and Experience file). Thresholds calibrated against real ingest of 35,998 rows on 2026-04-16. Spec §Zone 1 categories RL=12/RW=11/PT=9/OJ=9 (spec's original 'OJ=11' was a drafting error corrected after EDA). Per-occupation-x-scale sum observed max deviation is 0.03; tolerance 0.1 is safe. Occupation coverage is 878 O*NET-SOCs, not the spec's original ~1,016 -- range 800-1,100 accommodates future O*NET versions.

rules.name: onet_experience: row count 30,000-45,000
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 30000 OR cnt > 45000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_experience)
rules.description: Total row count must be in [30,000, 45,000]. O*NET ETE file covers 878 occupations x (12+11+9+9) = 35,998 rows in version 30.2. Range allows ~6K margin on each side for quarterly O*NET releases.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Row count: 35,998' (878 occupations x 41 scale-category slots). EDA recommendation: 'P0 rule 30,000 <= count <= 45,000 holds with wide margin.' Threshold: count in [30000, 45000].
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §Key Findings + §Edge Cases for DQ Thresholds

rules.name: onet_experience: onet_soc_code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE onet_soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code must match the O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX (two digits, hyphen, four digits, period, two digits). Deviation indicates a parsing or coercion failure in the ingestor.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'onet_soc_code format violations: 0 / 35,998.' 100% conformance to XX-XXXX.XX pattern observed on real data. Domain context declares this as the O*NET-SOC canonical format. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: scale_id in ('RL','RW','PT','OJ')
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE scale_id IS NULL OR scale_id NOT IN ('RL', 'RW', 'PT', 'OJ')
rules.description: scale_id must be exactly one of the four ETE scale codes: RL (Required Level of Education), RW (Related Work Experience), PT (On-Site or In-Plant Training), OJ (On-the-Job Training). Any other value indicates source format change or parsing error.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Exactly 4 scales present: RL (10,536), RW (9,658), PT (7,902), OJ (7,902).' Only these four values observed across all 35,998 rows. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: data_value range 0.0-100.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE data_value IS NULL OR data_value < 0.0 OR data_value > 100.0
rules.description: data_value represents a percent frequency and must fall within [0.0, 100.0]. Values outside this range indicate data corruption or parsing failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'data_value out-of-range values (< 0 or > 100): 0.' Observed range [0.00, 100.00]; per-scale max 95.83 (RW) to 100.00 (RL). All 35,998 rows within range. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: per (onet_soc_code, scale_id) sum equals 100 within tolerance 0.1
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, scale_id, SUM(data_value) AS group_sum FROM raw.onet_experience GROUP BY onet_soc_code, scale_id HAVING ABS(SUM(data_value) - 100.0) > 0.1
rules.description: For every (onet_soc_code, scale_id) group, the sum of data_value across all categories must equal 100 within tolerance +/- 0.1 (percent frequencies must form a complete distribution per scale per occupation).
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Per-(occupation x scale) frequency sum is uniformly ~100.0: mean 100.0004, std 0.008, max |dev| 0.03 across all 3,512 groups. Zero groups off by more than 1.0.' Real-data max deviation is 0.03, so 0.1 tolerance gives ~3x headroom. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: element_id NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE element_id IS NULL
rules.description: element_id is the Content Model element identifier (e.g., '3.A.1' for Related Work Experience) and must be populated on every row. Silver filters to element_id='3.A.1'; a null would silently drop occupations from Silver.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'element_id null rate: 0.00%' across 35,998 rows. Observed exact values: {2.D.1, 3.A.1, 3.A.2, 3.A.3} (1:1 with scale_id). Required field per spec Raw schema. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: grain uniqueness (onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category)
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_experience GROUP BY onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per (onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category). Any duplicate indicates an ingestor dedup failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: '(onet_soc_code, scale_id, category) is unique across the file (0 duplicates). The ingestor dedup grain of [onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category] is safe -- element_id is 1:1 with scale_id so adding it does not change uniqueness.' Spec §Zone 1 declares this grain. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: onet_experience: RW scale row count 9,000-12,500
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 9000 OR cnt > 12500 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE scale_id = 'RW')
rules.description: Row count for the RW (Related Work Experience) scale must be in [9,000, 12,500]. RW is the only scale consumed by Silver for experience-tier derivation.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'RW rows = 9,658' (878 occupations x 11 RW categories). EDA recommendation: 'rewrite P1 from 11,000-12,000 to 9,000 <= rw_rows <= 12,500 -- observed 9,658.' Range accommodates ~800-1,100 occupations x 11 categories across future O*NET versions. Threshold: count in [9000, 12500].
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §1. Row Counts by Scale + §Implications for DQ Rules

rules.name: onet_experience: occupation coverage 800-1,100 distinct onet_soc_codes
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 800 OR cnt > 1100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT onet_soc_code) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_experience)
rules.description: The number of distinct onet_soc_code values must be in [800, 1,100]. O*NET ETE has narrower occupation coverage than Occupation Data (878 vs 1,016) because ~138 recently-added/residual codes have no ETE survey yet.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Distinct onet_soc_code values: 878.' EDA recommendation: 'Bronze should not enforce 1,016 anywhere -- 878 occupations is the actual footprint.' Range 800-1,100 accommodates future O*NET releases where survey coverage may expand or contract. Threshold: distinct count in [800, 1100].

rules.name: onet_experience: per-scale distinct category counts RL=12, RW=11, PT=9, OJ=9
rules.sql: SELECT scale_id, COUNT(DISTINCT category) AS cat_count FROM raw.onet_experience GROUP BY scale_id HAVING (scale_id = 'RL' AND cat_count != 12) OR (scale_id = 'RW' AND cat_count != 11) OR (scale_id = 'PT' AND cat_count != 9) OR (scale_id = 'OJ' AND cat_count != 9)
rules.description: Each ETE scale must have its exact expected distinct category count: RL=12, RW=11, PT=9, OJ=9. Any deviation indicates a source format change or parsing failure. Note: spec's original 'OJ=11' was a drafting error corrected after EDA confirmed OJ=9.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Every one of the 878 occupations has all 4 scales with the exact spec-expected category count (RL=12, RW=11, PT=9, OJ=9). No partial-scale coverage anywhere.' EDA table 'Category Distribution per Scale' confirms observed vs expected match for RL/RW/PT, and flags OJ=9 as the corrected value (spec originally said 11). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: (scale_id, element_id) binding matches canonical pairs
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE NOT ((scale_id = 'RL' AND element_id = '2.D.1') OR (scale_id = 'RW' AND element_id = '3.A.1') OR (scale_id = 'PT' AND element_id = '3.A.2') OR (scale_id = 'OJ' AND element_id = '3.A.3'))
rules.description: Every row's (scale_id, element_id) pair must be in the canonical O*NET ETE set: ('RL','2.D.1'), ('RW','3.A.1'), ('PT','3.A.2'), ('OJ','3.A.3'). The 1:1 binding is a documented O*NET invariant; a mismatch would cause Silver's 'scale_id=RW AND element_id=3.A.1' filter to silently drop rows.
rules.rationale: EDA §Cross-Field Analysis: 'element_id is 1:1 with scale_id: RL<->2.D.1, RW<->3.A.1, PT<->3.A.2, OJ<->3.A.3.' 100% conformance across all 35,998 rows. Adversarial audit Gap A (CRITICAL) demonstrates that without this rule a scrambled mapping (probe P1) silently empties Silver -- rule 003 (scale_id in set) and rule 006 (element_id not null) both pass on that corruption. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: per-scale category value ENUM (RL 1-12, RW 1-11, PT 1-9, OJ 1-9)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE (scale_id = 'RL' AND (category < 1 OR category > 12)) OR (scale_id = 'RW' AND (category < 1 OR category > 11)) OR (scale_id = 'PT' AND (category < 1 OR category > 9)) OR (scale_id = 'OJ' AND (category < 1 OR category > 9))
rules.description: For each scale, category values must fall within the canonical range: RL in [1,12], RW in [1,11], PT in [1,9], OJ in [1,9]. Rule 010 checks COUNT(DISTINCT category) per scale but not the values themselves -- a substitution (e.g., replacing category=11 with category=15 in RW) preserves the distinct count but produces an out-of-range value with no midpoint in the Silver lookup table.
rules.rationale: EDA §3. Category Distribution per Scale: 'Min/max category per scale is [1, N] with no gaps' -- every occupation covers exactly 1..12 for RL, 1..11 for RW, 1..9 for PT, 1..9 for OJ. Spec §Zone 1 explicitly enumerates these ranges. Adversarial audit Gap B (HIGH) probe P6 demonstrates that rule 010 passes when a RW row is relabeled category=15. Single-rule compound-CASE style matches sibling rule 010. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: recommend_suppress in ('Y','N','n/a') and Y rate < 5%
rules.sql: SELECT 1 AS violation WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE recommend_suppress IS NOT NULL AND recommend_suppress NOT IN ('Y', 'N', 'n/a')) OR (SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN recommend_suppress = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) FROM raw.onet_experience) >= 0.05
rules.description: Two checks combined: (1) every non-null recommend_suppress value must be in ('Y','N','n/a'); (2) the rate of recommend_suppress='Y' across all rows must be strictly less than 5%. A blanket Y flip (probe P12) or a stray enum value (probe P13) would go undetected by the existing 10 rules.
rules.rationale: EDA §5. Suppression Rate: overall Y rate is 2.4% (864 / 35,998); per-scale max is PT at 2.9%. 5% ceiling gives ~2x headroom over the observed worst scale. Value set {N, n/a, Y} is exact (0 nulls, no other values). Adversarial audit Gap C (MEDIUM) probes P12 (flip every RW row to Y) and P13 (null a row) both pass all 10 existing rules today. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience: no (onet_soc_code, scale_id=RW) group has >=99% mass in a single category
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, scale_id, MAX(data_value) AS max_val FROM raw.onet_experience WHERE scale_id = 'RW' GROUP BY onet_soc_code, scale_id HAVING MAX(data_value) >= 99.0
rules.description: For each (onet_soc_code, scale_id) pair, MAX(data_value) must be < 99.0. A group with >=99% of probability mass in a single category is a pathologically collapsed distribution -- mass should be spread across multiple categories under normal O*NET ETE survey methodology.
rules.rationale: EDA §10. Weighted-Median Edge Cases: 'Zero RW rows have data_value = 100.0' and §4 data_value per-scale max is 95.83 (RW), 81.22 (PT), 90.40 (OJ). RL's per-scale max is exactly 100.00 on a small number of rows; check confirms at most ~1-2 RL groups approach this historically, and EDA text explicitly says '754 of 878 occupations (85.9%) have no single RW category above 50%' -- distributions are characteristically multi-modal. Adversarial audit Gap E (HIGH) probe P2 (collapse all RW to cat=1 at 100.0) would pass rules 004, 005, 010 today; the 99.0 threshold traps this 'all-entry-level' collapse while allowing the extreme-but-real chief-executive-style skew (68.24% at cat 11). Threshold: 0 violations.
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §4. data_value Sanity + §10. Weighted-Median Edge Cases
